# Design: contextLabeler — Ontology-Constrained AI Labeling for NVDA

**Generated by:** /office-hours on 2026-05-08
**Author:** Shubham Jakhete (shubhamsjakhete@gmail.com)
**Status:** APPROVED
**Mode:** Builder
**Target repo:** new standalone repo (proposed name: `nvda-context-labeler`)

---

## Executive Summary

`contextLabeler` is an NVDA add-on that fills in missing accessibility labels for unlabeled or ambiguous UI elements. When a blind user lands on an icon button or unlabeled image, they press a hotkey (`NVDA+Shift+L`) and the add-on speaks a meaningful label, generated by Claude Haiku and constrained by a hand-edited W3C ontology (RDF/Turtle, queried via SPARQL through RDFLib). Results are cached in memory. The full system is ~400 lines of original Python plus a vendored RDFLib.

The "explainable" angle: the ontology is a real, hand-readable Turtle file. Every classification Claude returns is validated against the ontology via a SPARQL `ASK` query before being spoken. No hallucinated categories. The user can open `ontology.ttl` and see the entire vocabulary the system is allowed to use.

---

## Problem Statement

Screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver) depend on application developers correctly labeling UI elements via `aria-label`, `alt`, accessible names, etc. In practice, large portions of modern apps fail this. When a blind user navigates to an unlabeled element, NVDA announces:

- *"button"* (no label, no context)
- *"graphic"* (no alt text)
- *"unlabeled"* (custom widget with broken role)

This is most painful in:
- Icon-only toolbars (Discord, Slack, Spotify, Figma)
- Custom web apps using `<div role="button">` without labels
- Decorative-but-functional images
- Electron apps that ignore platform a11y APIs

The user has no idea what the element does without trial-and-error activation, which is risky and slow.

---

## Goals (v0 scope)

1. Add-on installs into NVDA's Scratchpad mode for development, packages as `.nvda-addon` for distribution.
2. Hotkey `NVDA+Shift+L` triggers labeling of the currently focused element.
3. Add-on extracts text-only context from the focused `NVDAObject` (no screenshots in v0).
4. Context is sent to Anthropic Claude Haiku via REST.
5. Claude returns a JSON object with a category URI from the shipped ontology and a short freeform label.
6. Category URI is validated against the ontology via SPARQL before being accepted.
7. Validated label is spoken to the user via `ui.message()`.
8. Result is cached in memory keyed by element identity; subsequent presses on the same element are instant.
9. Anthropic API key is configurable through NVDA's standard settings panel UI.
10. Errors (network failure, invalid JSON, hallucinated category) degrade gracefully — the add-on speaks a fallback message and never crashes NVDA.

## Non-goals (deferred to v1+)

- Screenshots / vision API
- Auto-trigger on focus events (hotkey only)
- Per-app override files
- Persistent disk cache
- Local model fallback
- Telemetry / analytics
- Multi-language support (en-US only for v0)
- Customizable hotkey (hardcoded `NVDA+Shift+L`)
- Multi-element batch labeling

---

## Architecture Overview

```
[user presses NVDA+Shift+L]
            |
            v
   GlobalPlugin.script_labelFocused()              ← __init__.py
            |
            v
   focused = api.getFocusObject()                   ← NVDA core
            |
            v
   ctx = context.extract(focused)                   ← context.py
   {role, name, class, parent_name, sibling_names, window_title, app_name}
            |
            v
   key = cache.make_key(ctx)
   cached = cache.lookup(key)
            |
   +--------+--------+
   |                 |
  hit               miss
   |                 |
   |                 v
   |         result = classifier.classify(ctx)      ← classifier.py
   |                 |    (HTTPS POST to api.anthropic.com)
   |                 v
   |         {category_uri, label}
   |                 |
   |                 v
   |         valid = ontology.validate_leaf(uri)    ← queries.py + RDFLib
   |                 |
   |          +------+------+
   |          |             |
   |        valid         invalid
   |          |             |
   |          v             v
   |   speakable = ontology.label_for(uri)     fallback msg
   |          |             |
   |          v             |
   |   cache.store(key, result)                |
   |          |             |
   +----------+-------------+
              |
              v
   ui.message(speakable)                            ← NVDA speaks
```

---

## Tech Stack (final)

| Layer | Choice | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Host | NVDA 2024.1 or newer (Windows) | Runtime; user installs separately |
| Language | Python 3.11 (NVDA bundled) | No system Python required |
| Add-on type | Global plugin | `globalPluginHandler.GlobalPlugin` subclass |
| NVDA APIs used | `globalPluginHandler`, `api`, `ui`, `scriptHandler`, `gui.settingsDialogs`, `config`, `addonHandler`, `logHandler` | All in NVDA's bundled module set |
| Ontology format | RDF/Turtle (`.ttl`) | W3C standard, hand-readable |
| Ontology library | RDFLib 7.x (vendored) | Pure Python, no native deps |
| Query language | SPARQL 1.1 (via RDFLib) | Validation + hierarchy walks |
| AI provider | Anthropic Claude (`claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` recommended) | Fast (~400-800 ms), cheap, JSON-mode capable |
| HTTP client | `urllib.request` (stdlib) | Avoids vendoring requests; sufficient for one POST endpoint |
| JSON | `json` (stdlib) | For request/response only; ontology is Turtle |
| Cache | In-memory `dict` | Resets on NVDA restart; no persistence in v0 |
| Settings UI | `gui.settingsDialogs.SettingsPanel` | Standard NVDA pattern |
| Settings storage | NVDA `config.conf` via `config.conf` API | Encrypted at rest? No — plain text on disk; documented limitation |
| Packaging | Zip + `.nvda-addon` extension | Matches NVDA add-on store format |
| Dev environment | NVDA Scratchpad (`%APPDATA%\nvda\scratchpad\`) | No build step during dev |

### Vendored dependencies (committed to repo)

- `rdflib` (~1.5 MB) — pure Python
- `pyparsing` (~250 KB) — RDFLib parser dep
- `isodate` (~50 KB) — RDFLib date parsing dep

Total add-on bundle: ~2 MB.

---

## Repository Structure

Target repo name: `nvda-context-labeler` (new standalone repo, not a fork of NVDA).

```
nvda-context-labeler/
├── README.md                              # user-facing: what it does, install steps
├── DESIGN.md                              # this document (or symlink to it)
├── LICENSE                                # MIT recommended
├── .gitignore                             # standard Python + NVDA add-on
├── manifest.ini                           # NVDA add-on metadata (root of zip)
├── globalPlugins/
│   └── contextLabeler/
│       ├── __init__.py                    # GlobalPlugin entry, hotkey, orchestration
│       ├── context.py                     # NVDAObject → context dict
│       ├── classifier.py                  # Claude API wrapper
│       ├── queries.py                     # SPARQL query string constants
│       ├── ontology.py                    # RDFLib graph wrapper + SPARQL execution
│       ├── ontology.ttl                   # the ontology itself (Turtle)
│       ├── cache.py                       # in-memory cache
│       ├── settings.py                    # NVDA settings panel
│       └── _vendor/                       # vendored deps (see "Vendoring")
│           ├── __init__.py                # empty; marks package
│           ├── rdflib/                    # copied from `pip install --target`
│           ├── pyparsing/
│           └── isodate/
├── tests/
│   ├── test-bad-a11y.html                 # browser test page with broken UI
│   └── test_ontology.py                   # standalone ontology validation tests
├── docs/
│   ├── developer-setup.md                 # how to set up Windows dev env
│   ├── ontology-guide.md                  # how to extend the ontology
│   └── architecture.md                    # explains the pipeline
└── scripts/
    ├── vendor-deps.sh                     # runs the pip --target vendoring
    └── package-addon.sh                   # zips + renames to .nvda-addon
```

---

## The Ontology (`globalPlugins/contextLabeler/ontology.ttl`)

Hand-edited Turtle file. Ships with the add-on. ~50-80 lines for v0.

```turtle
@prefix : <http://contextlabeler.org/ui-ontology#> .
@prefix rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> .
@prefix owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#> .
@prefix xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#> .

# Root
:UIElement a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:label "UI element"@en ;
    rdfs:comment "Any user interface element a screen reader might encounter."@en .

# === Controls ===
:Control a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :UIElement ;
    rdfs:label "control"@en .

:Button a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Control ;
    rdfs:label "button"@en .

:CommandButton a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Button ;
    rdfs:label "command button"@en ;
    rdfs:comment "Button with text that performs an action when activated."@en .

:IconButton a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Button ;
    rdfs:label "icon button"@en ;
    rdfs:comment "Button shown as an icon with no visible text label."@en .

:ActionIconButton a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :IconButton ;
    rdfs:label "action icon button"@en ;
    rdfs:comment "Icon button that performs a one-shot action (save, send, delete)."@en .

:ToggleIconButton a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :IconButton ;
    rdfs:label "toggle icon button"@en ;
    rdfs:comment "Icon button representing a binary state (mute/unmute, play/pause)."@en .

:Input a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Control ;
    rdfs:label "input field"@en .

:TextInput a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Input ;
    rdfs:label "text input"@en .

:SearchInput a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Input ;
    rdfs:label "search input"@en .

:Selector a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Control ;
    rdfs:label "selector"@en .

:Dropdown a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Selector ;
    rdfs:label "dropdown"@en .

:Checkbox a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Selector ;
    rdfs:label "checkbox"@en .

:RadioButton a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Selector ;
    rdfs:label "radio button"@en .

# === Display ===
:Display a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :UIElement ;
    rdfs:label "display element"@en .

:Image a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Display ;
    rdfs:label "image"@en .

:DecorativeImage a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Image ;
    rdfs:label "decorative image"@en ;
    rdfs:comment "Image that conveys no information; safe to ignore."@en .

:InformativeImage a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Image ;
    rdfs:label "informative image"@en ;
    rdfs:comment "Image that conveys meaningful content (chart, photo, diagram)."@en .

:FunctionalImage a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Image ;
    rdfs:label "functional image"@en ;
    rdfs:comment "Image that acts as a clickable control."@en .

:Icon a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Display ;
    rdfs:label "icon"@en .

:StatusIcon a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Icon ;
    rdfs:label "status icon"@en ;
    rdfs:comment "Icon indicating state (online, error, loading)."@en .

:BrandIcon a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Icon ;
    rdfs:label "brand icon"@en ;
    rdfs:comment "Logo or brand mark."@en .

# === Navigation ===
:Navigation a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :UIElement ;
    rdfs:label "navigation element"@en .

:Link a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Navigation ;
    rdfs:label "link"@en .

:Tab a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Navigation ;
    rdfs:label "tab"@en .

:MenuItem a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :Navigation ;
    rdfs:label "menu item"@en .

# === Unknown sentinel ===
:Unknown a owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf :UIElement ;
    rdfs:label "unknown element"@en ;
    rdfs:comment "Fallback when the element does not fit any other category."@en .
```

The agent should use this exact content as the v0 ontology. It's intentionally narrow; expanding it is a v1 concern.

---

## Component Specifications

### `manifest.ini` (root of zip)

```ini
name = "contextLabeler"
summary = "Context Labeler"
description = "Uses an ontology and Claude AI to label unlabeled UI elements."
version = "0.1.0"
author = "Shubham Jakhete <shubhamsjakhete@gmail.com>"
url = "https://github.com/<TBD>/nvda-context-labeler"
minimumNVDAVersion = "2024.1"
lastTestedNVDAVersion = "2024.4"
```

### `globalPlugins/contextLabeler/__init__.py`

Responsibilities:
- Inject `_vendor/` into `sys.path` BEFORE any other imports from this package
- Define `GlobalPlugin(globalPluginHandler.GlobalPlugin)` subclass
- Register the settings panel on plugin load
- Bind the `NVDA+Shift+L` gesture via `__gestures` dict
- Implement `script_labelFocused(self, gesture)`: orchestrates the pipeline
- Speak progress feedback ("Labeling...") if classification takes >300ms
- Catch all exceptions; log via `logHandler.log.error` and speak a fallback

Skeleton:

```python
import os, sys
_VENDOR = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "_vendor")
if _VENDOR not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, _VENDOR)

import globalPluginHandler
import api
import ui
import scriptHandler
from logHandler import log
import gui

from . import context, classifier, cache, settings
from .ontology import Ontology

class GlobalPlugin(globalPluginHandler.GlobalPlugin):
    scriptCategory = "Context Labeler"

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self._ontology = Ontology.load_default()
        self._cache = cache.Cache()
        gui.settingsDialogs.NVDASettingsDialog.categoryClasses.append(
            settings.ContextLabelerPanel
        )

    def terminate(self):
        gui.settingsDialogs.NVDASettingsDialog.categoryClasses.remove(
            settings.ContextLabelerPanel
        )

    @scriptHandler.script(
        description="Label the currently focused element using AI + ontology",
        gesture="kb:NVDA+shift+l",
    )
    def script_labelFocused(self, gesture):
        try:
            obj = api.getFocusObject()
            ctx = context.extract(obj)
            key = cache.make_key(ctx)
            cached = self._cache.lookup(key)
            if cached:
                ui.message(cached)
                return
            api_key = settings.get_api_key()
            if not api_key:
                ui.message("Context Labeler: API key not set in settings")
                return
            allowed_uris = self._ontology.leaf_uris()
            result = classifier.classify(ctx, allowed_uris, api_key)
            if not self._ontology.is_valid_leaf(result["category"]):
                log.warning(f"Claude returned invalid category: {result['category']}")
                ui.message(f"unlabeled element — could not classify")
                return
            human_category = self._ontology.label_for(result["category"])
            speakable = f"{result['label']} — {human_category}"
            self._cache.store(key, speakable)
            ui.message(speakable)
        except Exception as e:
            log.error("contextLabeler error", exc_info=True)
            ui.message("Context Labeler: error — see NVDA log")
```

### `globalPlugins/contextLabeler/context.py`

Pure function. No I/O. Easy to unit test.

```python
def extract(obj) -> dict:
    """
    Extract text-only context from an NVDAObject.
    Returns a dict with these keys (any may be empty string):
      - role: human name of the role (e.g. "button", "graphic")
      - name: accessible name (often empty; that's why we're here)
      - description: longer accessible description
      - window_class: windowClassName
      - window_text: windowText
      - app_name: app module name (e.g. "discord")
      - parent_name: name of parent object, if any
      - parent_role: role of parent
      - sibling_names: list of up to 5 siblings' names (only non-empty ones)
      - window_title: title of the foreground window
    Never raises; returns empty strings on any AttributeError.
    """
```

Implementation notes for the agent:
- Use `controlTypes.roleLabels[obj.role]` for human role name
- Use `obj.appModule.appName` for app name
- Walk siblings via `obj.parent.children` and filter for non-empty names
- Wrap each access in try/except (NVDAObject attribute access can throw on dead objects)
- Cap sibling list at 5 entries to keep prompt size predictable

### `globalPlugins/contextLabeler/classifier.py`

```python
import json
import urllib.request

ANTHROPIC_API_URL = "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages"
MODEL = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 8

SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = """You are classifying UI elements for a screen reader user.

Your task: given metadata about a focused UI element that has no accessible label,
return a JSON object with two fields:
  - "category": one of the URIs from the allowed list below
  - "label": a short (1-4 word) human-readable label

Allowed category URIs (you MUST pick one of these exactly):
{allowed_uris}

Rules:
- Output ONLY a single JSON object. No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- "label" must be lowercase, no punctuation.
- If you cannot determine a label, use category ":Unknown" and label "unlabeled element".
"""

USER_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = """Element metadata:
- Role: {role}
- Name: {name}
- Description: {description}
- Window class: {window_class}
- Window text: {window_text}
- App: {app_name}
- Parent role: {parent_role}
- Parent name: {parent_name}
- Sibling labels: {sibling_names}
- Window title: {window_title}

Classify this element."""

def classify(ctx: dict, allowed_uris: list[str], api_key: str) -> dict:
    """
    Call Claude. Return {"category": URI, "label": str}.
    Raises on network failure or invalid JSON; caller handles.
    """
    system = SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE.format(allowed_uris="\n".join(allowed_uris))
    user = USER_PROMPT_TEMPLATE.format(**ctx)
    body = json.dumps({
        "model": MODEL,
        "max_tokens": 200,
        "system": system,
        "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": user}],
    }).encode("utf-8")
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        ANTHROPIC_API_URL,
        data=body,
        headers={
            "x-api-key": api_key,
            "anthropic-version": "2023-06-01",
            "content-type": "application/json",
        },
        method="POST",
    )
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=TIMEOUT_SECONDS) as resp:
        payload = json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8"))
    text = payload["content"][0]["text"].strip()
    return json.loads(text)
```

### `globalPlugins/contextLabeler/ontology.py`

```python
import os
from rdflib import Graph, RDFS, URIRef

ONTOLOGY_PATH = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "ontology.ttl")
NS = "http://contextlabeler.org/ui-ontology#"

class Ontology:
    def __init__(self, graph: Graph):
        self._g = graph

    @classmethod
    def load_default(cls) -> "Ontology":
        g = Graph()
        g.parse(ONTOLOGY_PATH, format="turtle")
        return cls(g)

    def leaf_uris(self) -> list[str]:
        """All classes that have no subclasses (the categories Claude can pick)."""
        from .queries import LEAVES
        return [str(row.leaf) for row in self._g.query(LEAVES)]

    def is_valid_leaf(self, uri: str) -> bool:
        from .queries import IS_LEAF
        result = self._g.query(IS_LEAF, initBindings={"leaf": URIRef(uri)})
        return bool(result.askAnswer)

    def label_for(self, uri: str) -> str:
        label = self._g.value(URIRef(uri), RDFS.label)
        return str(label) if label else uri.rsplit("#", 1)[-1]

    def ancestors(self, uri: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
        """Returns [(uri, label), ...] from immediate parent up to root."""
        from .queries import ANCESTORS
        return [
            (str(row.ancestor), str(row.label))
            for row in self._g.query(ANCESTORS, initBindings={"node": URIRef(uri)})
        ]
```

### `globalPlugins/contextLabeler/queries.py`

Plain string constants. Keeps SPARQL out of the orchestration code.

```python
PREFIXES = """
PREFIX : <http://contextlabeler.org/ui-ontology#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
"""

LEAVES = PREFIXES + """
SELECT ?leaf WHERE {
  ?leaf a owl:Class .
  ?leaf rdfs:subClassOf+ :UIElement .
  FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?child rdfs:subClassOf ?leaf }
}
"""

IS_LEAF = PREFIXES + """
ASK {
  ?leaf a owl:Class .
  ?leaf rdfs:subClassOf+ :UIElement .
  FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?child rdfs:subClassOf ?leaf }
}
"""

ANCESTORS = PREFIXES + """
SELECT ?ancestor ?label WHERE {
  ?node rdfs:subClassOf+ ?ancestor .
  ?ancestor rdfs:label ?label .
}
"""
```

### `globalPlugins/contextLabeler/cache.py`

```python
class Cache:
    def __init__(self, max_entries: int = 500):
        self._data: dict[str, str] = {}
        self._max = max_entries

    def lookup(self, key: str) -> str | None:
        return self._data.get(key)

    def store(self, key: str, value: str) -> None:
        if len(self._data) >= self._max:
            self._data.pop(next(iter(self._data)))
        self._data[key] = value

def make_key(ctx: dict) -> str:
    """Stable key for caching. Includes the fields that uniquely identify
    a UI element across focus events within an app."""
    return "|".join([
        ctx.get("app_name", ""),
        ctx.get("window_class", ""),
        ctx.get("role", ""),
        ctx.get("parent_name", ""),
        # window_text often distinguishes elements within the same class
        ctx.get("window_text", "")[:50],
    ])
```

### `globalPlugins/contextLabeler/settings.py`

```python
import wx
import config
import gui
from gui.settingsDialogs import SettingsPanel

CONFIG_SECTION = "contextLabeler"
CONFIG_SPEC = {
    "apiKey": "string(default='')",
    "logClassifications": "boolean(default=False)",
}

# Register config spec on import
config.conf.spec[CONFIG_SECTION] = CONFIG_SPEC

def get_api_key() -> str:
    return config.conf[CONFIG_SECTION]["apiKey"]

def get_log_classifications() -> bool:
    return config.conf[CONFIG_SECTION]["logClassifications"]

class ContextLabelerPanel(SettingsPanel):
    title = "Context Labeler"

    def makeSettings(self, settingsSizer):
        sizer = gui.guiHelper.BoxSizerHelper(self, sizer=settingsSizer)
        self._apiKey = sizer.addLabeledControl("Anthropic API key:", wx.TextCtrl)
        self._apiKey.SetValue(config.conf[CONFIG_SECTION]["apiKey"])
        self._logToggle = sizer.addItem(wx.CheckBox(self, label="Log classifications to NVDA log"))
        self._logToggle.SetValue(config.conf[CONFIG_SECTION]["logClassifications"])

    def onSave(self):
        config.conf[CONFIG_SECTION]["apiKey"] = self._apiKey.GetValue()
        config.conf[CONFIG_SECTION]["logClassifications"] = self._logToggle.GetValue()
```

---

## The Claude API Contract

**Endpoint:** `POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages`

**Headers:**
- `x-api-key: <user's key from settings>`
- `anthropic-version: 2023-06-01`
- `content-type: application/json`

**Request body:**
```json
{
  "model": "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
  "max_tokens": 200,
  "system": "<SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE filled with allowed URIs>",
  "messages": [
    {"role": "user", "content": "<USER_PROMPT_TEMPLATE filled with context>"}
  ]
}
```

**Expected response shape:**
```json
{
  "content": [
    {"type": "text", "text": "{\"category\": \"http://contextlabeler.org/ui-ontology#ActionIconButton\", \"label\": \"send message\"}"}
  ]
}
```

**Failure modes the code must handle:**
1. Network timeout (8s) → `ui.message("Context Labeler: network timeout")`
2. HTTP 401 (bad API key) → `ui.message("Context Labeler: invalid API key")`
3. HTTP 429 (rate limited) → `ui.message("Context Labeler: rate limited, try again")`
4. Response not valid JSON → fallback message + log
5. JSON missing required fields → fallback message + log
6. `category` field not in allowed URIs → fallback message + log warning
7. Any other exception → caught at the script level, logged, `ui.message("error — see NVDA log")`

---

## Vendoring Procedure

Run once on any machine with Python 3.11 and pip. Output gets committed to the repo.

`scripts/vendor-deps.sh`:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
VENDOR_DIR="globalPlugins/contextLabeler/_vendor"
rm -rf "$VENDOR_DIR"
mkdir -p "$VENDOR_DIR"
pip install --target="$VENDOR_DIR" --no-deps rdflib==7.0.0
pip install --target="$VENDOR_DIR" --no-deps pyparsing
pip install --target="$VENDOR_DIR" --no-deps isodate
touch "$VENDOR_DIR/__init__.py"
echo "Vendored deps into $VENDOR_DIR"
```

(Windows equivalent: `scripts/vendor-deps.ps1` — same calls, PowerShell syntax.)

---

## Setup Procedure (Windows dev box)

Documented in `docs/developer-setup.md`:

1. Install NVDA 2024.1+ from `nvaccess.org`.
2. Install Python 3.11 (only needed once, for vendoring; not for running NVDA).
3. Clone the repo: `git clone https://github.com/<user>/nvda-context-labeler.git`
4. Run vendoring: `bash scripts/vendor-deps.sh` (or the .ps1 on Windows).
5. Symlink (or copy) the add-on into NVDA's scratchpad:
   - Scratchpad path: `%APPDATA%\nvda\scratchpad\globalPlugins\contextLabeler`
   - PowerShell symlink: `New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path "$env:APPDATA\nvda\scratchpad\globalPlugins\contextLabeler" -Target "<repo>\globalPlugins\contextLabeler"`
6. In NVDA: Preferences → Settings → Advanced → enable "Enable loading of custom code from the Developer Scratchpad Directory".
7. Restart NVDA (or `NVDA+Ctrl+F3` to reload plugins).
8. In NVDA: Preferences → Settings → Context Labeler → paste Anthropic API key.
9. Open `tests/test-bad-a11y.html` in any browser.
10. Tab to an unlabeled element, press `NVDA+Shift+L`.

---

## Test Procedure

### Unit-level (runs on Mac/Linux, not in NVDA)

`tests/test_ontology.py` — pure Python, no NVDA, runnable with `python -m unittest`:

```python
import unittest
import sys, os
sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "..", "globalPlugins", "contextLabeler", "_vendor"))
sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "..", "globalPlugins"))
from contextLabeler.ontology import Ontology

class TestOntology(unittest.TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.ont = Ontology.load_default()

    def test_loads_without_error(self):
        self.assertIsNotNone(self.ont)

    def test_has_leaves(self):
        leaves = self.ont.leaf_uris()
        self.assertGreater(len(leaves), 5)

    def test_known_leaf_validates(self):
        uri = "http://contextlabeler.org/ui-ontology#ActionIconButton"
        self.assertTrue(self.ont.is_valid_leaf(uri))

    def test_made_up_uri_does_not_validate(self):
        uri = "http://contextlabeler.org/ui-ontology#FakeCategory"
        self.assertFalse(self.ont.is_valid_leaf(uri))

    def test_label_for_known_leaf(self):
        uri = "http://contextlabeler.org/ui-ontology#ActionIconButton"
        self.assertEqual(self.ont.label_for(uri), "action icon button")
```

### Integration test (manual, on Windows)

`tests/test-bad-a11y.html`:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head><title>Context Labeler Test Page</title><meta charset="utf-8"></head>
<body>
  <h1>NVDA Context Labeler — Test Page</h1>

  <h2>Unlabeled icon buttons</h2>
  <button class="btn-save"><span aria-hidden="true">💾</span></button>
  <button class="btn-trash"><span aria-hidden="true">🗑</span></button>
  <button class="btn-share"><span aria-hidden="true">📤</span></button>
  <button class="btn-mute"><span aria-hidden="true">🔇</span></button>

  <h2>Images with no alt</h2>
  <img src="https://placehold.co/100" width="100" height="100">
  <img src="https://placehold.co/200x60?text=brand" width="200" height="60">

  <h2>Ambiguous buttons</h2>
  <button>Click here</button>
  <button>Submit</button>
  <button>OK</button>

  <h2>Custom widgets pretending to be buttons</h2>
  <div class="upload-button" tabindex="0" role="button"><span>⬆</span></div>

  <h2>Properly labeled (control)</h2>
  <button aria-label="Save document">💾</button>
</body>
</html>
```

Manual test matrix: tab to each element, press `NVDA+Shift+L`, verify the spoken output is plausibly correct.

---

## Acceptance Criteria for v0

The build is "done" when ALL of these hold:

- [ ] Repo builds and vendoring script completes without errors on a clean machine.
- [ ] `python -m unittest tests/test_ontology.py` passes 100%.
- [ ] Add-on loads in NVDA scratchpad without errors (check NVDA log on startup).
- [ ] "Context Labeler" panel appears under NVDA Preferences → Settings.
- [ ] Pressing `NVDA+Shift+L` with no API key set speaks the "API key not set" message.
- [ ] With a valid API key, pressing `NVDA+Shift+L` on an unlabeled button on `test-bad-a11y.html` produces a plausibly correct spoken label within 2 seconds.
- [ ] Pressing `NVDA+Shift+L` a second time on the same element speaks instantly (cache hit).
- [ ] If Claude returns a malformed/hallucinated category, NVDA speaks the fallback message and does not crash.
- [ ] If the network is down, NVDA speaks the timeout message after ~8 seconds and does not crash.
- [ ] `scripts/package-addon.sh` produces a valid `contextLabeler-0.1.0.nvda-addon` file that installs via NVDA's Add-on Store GUI.

---

## Build Phases (suggested order for the implementing agent)

The agent picking up this design should build in this order. Each phase ends with a verification step.

### Phase 1: Skeleton + manifest (1-2 hours)
- Create the repo structure
- Write `manifest.ini`, empty plugin files, `.gitignore`, `LICENSE`, `README.md`
- Write a stub `__init__.py` that just logs "contextLabeler loaded" on startup
- **Verify:** install in scratchpad, restart NVDA, see the log line

### Phase 2: Hello-world hotkey (30 min)
- Implement `script_labelFocused` to just speak `obj.role` and `obj.windowClassName`
- **Verify:** open Notepad, press hotkey, hear "edit, NotepadEditWindow" or similar

### Phase 3: Vendor RDFLib + load ontology (1 hour)
- Run vendoring script
- Write `ontology.ttl` (full v0 content from this doc)
- Write `ontology.py` with `load_default()`, `leaf_uris()`, `is_valid_leaf()`, `label_for()`
- Write `queries.py`
- Write `tests/test_ontology.py`
- **Verify:** `python -m unittest tests/test_ontology.py` passes

### Phase 4: Settings panel (1 hour)
- Implement `settings.py`
- Register panel in `__init__.py`
- **Verify:** panel shows up, API key persists across NVDA restart

### Phase 5: Context extraction (1 hour)
- Implement `context.py`
- Update `script_labelFocused` to extract context and speak it raw (debug mode)
- **Verify:** open Discord, focus an icon, see all relevant fields populated in the spoken output

### Phase 6: Claude classifier (2 hours)
- Implement `classifier.py` with full prompt templates
- Wire into `script_labelFocused`
- Implement validation against ontology
- **Verify:** real classification works on `test-bad-a11y.html`

### Phase 7: Cache + error handling (1 hour)
- Implement `cache.py`
- Wrap pipeline in try/except with graceful fallbacks for each error mode
- **Verify:** all entries in the Acceptance Criteria checklist pass

### Phase 8: Packaging + docs (1 hour)
- Write `package-addon.sh`
- Write `docs/developer-setup.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/ontology-guide.md`
- **Verify:** `.nvda-addon` file installs correctly via the NVDA Add-on Store GUI

**Total estimated effort: ~8-10 hours of focused work.**

---

## Open Questions

These do NOT block v0 but should be tracked for v1:

1. **Privacy notice.** Sending app names + window titles to a third party may surprise users. Should v0 show a one-time consent dialog on first activation? *Recommendation: yes — implement before any public release, can ship without for personal use.*
2. **Latency budget.** Is 800ms acceptable on a hotkey press? Should we play a tick sound after 200ms to reassure the user that we're working? *Recommendation: defer to v1 after measuring real-world latency.*
3. **Ontology versioning.** When the ontology changes, should cached results be invalidated? *Recommendation: cache key includes ontology hash; out of scope for v0 since cache is in-memory anyway.*
4. **Multi-modal.** When v1 adds screenshots, do we change the ontology contract or keep it the same? *Recommendation: keep contract identical; vision is just another input to the same prompt.*

---

## What I noticed about how you think (per office-hours convention)

- You started with "build a small prototype" but pivoted, unprompted, to a real accessibility problem the moment we explored it. That's the move people who actually ship things make — they don't stay with the toy idea.
- You explicitly asked to upgrade the stack from JSON to "Python + RDFLib + RDF/Turtle + SPARQL" mid-design. That's a sign you care about the *credibility* of the artifact, not just whether it works. Most builders cut corners on the part nobody sees; you upgraded it.
- Your questions stayed practical (*"how do I run this? what do I open?"*) without ever drifting into hypothetical product strategy. That's a builder reading: ship something real, see it work, then think.

---

## Glossary

- **NVDA** — NonVisual Desktop Access, an open-source Windows screen reader.
- **NVDAObject** — NVDA's runtime representation of a single UI element. Has properties like `role`, `name`, `parent`, `location`.
- **Global plugin** — NVDA add-on that runs in all apps, typically driven by hotkeys.
- **Scratchpad** — NVDA dev mode that loads add-on code from `%APPDATA%\nvda\scratchpad\` without requiring a packaged `.nvda-addon`.
- **RDF** — Resource Description Framework, W3C standard for representing knowledge as subject-predicate-object triples.
- **Turtle (TTL)** — Compact, hand-readable serialization format for RDF.
- **SPARQL** — Query language for RDF graphs (think SQL for triples).
- **OWL** — Web Ontology Language, builds on RDF/RDFS to express classes, properties, and reasoning rules.
- **Leaf class** — In a class hierarchy, a class with no subclasses. The categories Claude can return.
- **rdfs:subClassOf+** — In SPARQL, the `+` makes the relationship transitive: any class anywhere up the chain.
